$comment: Multi-Agent Example — Demonstrates the `subAgents` configuration.
This agent acts as a parent that delegates to two remote A2A sub-agents
by exposing them as MCP tools via `a2a-mcp-skillmap`. The bridge is
spawned automatically as a stdio MCP server and registered under the
reserved `a2a-subagents` key in the resolved `mcp` map.
Sub-agents declared here become callable as ordinary MCP tools by
OpenCode. The LLM sees `coding__<skill>` and `research__<skill>` tools
(one per skill advertised in each sub-agent's card) and can dispatch
to them like any other MCP tool.
Two illustrative entries below:
- `coding`   — uses `${EXAMPLE_TOKEN}` env-var substitution for auth
- `research` — uses `endpointUrlOverride` to point the bridge at an
internal URL that differs from the agent card's public URL

agentCard.name: Multi-Agent Orchestrator
agentCard.description: Parent agent that delegates to coding and research sub-agents via the a2a-mcp-skillmap bridge.

skills.name: Multi-Agent Orchestrator
skills.description: Routes user requests to the most appropriate sub-agent and synthesizes responses

opencode.systemPrompt: You are an orchestrator agent. Inspect available tools and delegate user requests to the most appropriate sub-agent. Synthesize their responses into a single, coherent answer for the user.

$comment: Each entry exposes a remote A2A agent as MCP tools.
name              — Required. Stable identifier prefixed onto generated tool names.
Allowed chars: letters, digits, hyphens, underscores. Must be unique.
agentCardUrl      — Required. URL of the sub-agent's A2A agent card.
Typically https://<host>/.well-known/agent-card.json
endpointUrlOverride — Optional. Override for the URL the bridge probes when
the agent card declares an endpoint that differs from
where the agent is actually reachable (NAT, port-forward,
internal vs external).
auth              — Optional. Outbound credentials forwarded to the sub-agent.
Modes: 'none' | 'bearer' | 'api_key'.
Tokens may reference env vars via ${VAR_NAME} syntax.
Missing env vars produce a startup warning and the auth
block is omitted (the bridge calls without credentials).
options.responseMode  — Skillmap response shaping mode. Default 'artifact'.
options.probeTimeoutMs — Startup reachability probe timeout. Default 5000.
options.syncBudgetMs  — How long (ms) the bridge waits for an A2A response
before returning a task handle for async polling.
Default 30000. Set to 0 to wait indefinitely.
